steps.title: Compute the result
steps.content: Simplify: $$f(7) = 21 - 8 = 13.$$ That matches choice B — B is correct.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice B is correct. It's given that the function $f$ is defined by $f\left(x\right)=3x-8$. The value of $f\left(7\right)$ is the value of $f\left(x\right)$ when $x=7$. Substituting $7$ for $x$ in the given equation yields $f\left(7\right)=3\left(7\right)-8$, which is equivalent to $f\left(7\right)=21-8$, or $f\left(7\right)=13$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice A is incorrect. This is the value of $f\left(7\right)$ when $f\left(x\right)=3x+8$, rather than $f\left(x\right)=3x-8$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice C is incorrect. This is the value of $f\left(1\right)$, rather than $f\left(7\right)$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice D is incorrect. This is the value of $f\left(-7\right)$, rather than $f\left(7\right)$.
